transcript:
Wow. They weren't lying when they called it absolute cinema, this stage. Um, you've heard all... all of you heard this a thousand times, right? And in 2024 alone, and for good reason, in 2024 alone, 9.9 billion dollars was stolen from users because they... simply because they willingly or unwillingly shared their seed phrases. Every person who lost funds or knows someone who did is far less likely to return to all of this. And while it's much harder to fight social engineering scams where users willingly but unconsciously share their seed phrases, we can drastically reduce the rest. How? With hardware... with hardware wallets.

The primary job of a hardware wallet is to keep your keys in an offline chip and sign transactions without ever exposing them. This drastically increases the security of your assets, and that's why they have become a staple of this industry. Today's hardware wallets though have expanded far beyond their primary job. And that kind of became a problem. As you see, most hardware wallets want to be supported by all software wallets and on many chains. And then they need to compensate for some of the weaknesses of these software wallets.

As they... uh... there are wallets that... because there are wallets out there that unlike Solflare don't have reliable transaction simulations or constant threat monitoring, and hardware wallet companies stepped in to fix that. That's why clear signing is the number one marketed benefit of hardware wallet companies today. Something that Solflare users get by default. So what does this extra job they're doing actually require from them?

Unnecessary complexity. An offline chip needs to become a complex device. That has a screen, that needs to charge, that needs to be connected via cable or Bluetooth, and that's not truly portable. More device also means more things that can break, and lifetime degrades drastically. And having a whole device also makes it unnecessarily expensive. So just because of the shortcomings of some software wallets, all of us here are paying the price.

And that price becomes an even bigger problem. When a safe crypto journey starts at 150 dollars, countless people are shut out before they can even begin. And that's completely missing the point. We believe there's a better way. And that's why I can present to you the Solflare Shield.

[Video plays showcasing the Solflare Shield card]

The Solflare Shield provides peak security with great UX at an affordable price. The Solflare Shield does not need to compensate for other software wallets' shortcomings as it works exclusively with Solflare, so it only needs to do its primary job. It can stay an offline chip that signs transactions without ever exposing your private keys. It's that mighty simple. There are no screens, no cables, no Bluetooth, no batteries, and it's truly portable. It fits in your back pocket at all times.

Your keys are stored in an offline... in an offline chip of the highest security standards, and using it is as easy as tapping your Shield to the back of your phone. Lifetime? You're getting a 25-year warranty. And all of that starts at 49 dollars. Uncompromising security comes at the cost of two movie tickets and popcorn, or just half a cent a day over its lifetime. For 59 dollars, you can get a Signature edition with designs from talented Solana artists. For 69 dollars, you get two Shields. For 79 dollars, you get a fully personalized Shield that matches your style.

If you pre-order in the next two weeks, you'll also get an awesome promo deal. And before you head over to our webshop, there's one more thing to tell. Shipping starts now. You all get a free Shield. So every Breakpoint attendee just needs to head over to our booth that is just outside and collect their free Shield. You all deserve a mighty simple personal Shield. Thank you.

# Solflare unveils self-custody crypto debit card
Today, Solflare, the gateway to the next era of finance, has partnered with Mastercard, to launch the Solflare Card, the first true self-custody debit card on Solana.
[]()019 November 2025[Be the first to comment](#comments)
#### External
This content is provided by an external author without editing by Finextra. It expresses the views and opinions of the author.
Seamlessly linking to users' Solflare Wallets, the new digital card allows users to make instant debit card payments with USDC while maintaining full control of their assets, without preloading or prior conversion. The Solflare card will initially be available to those in the UK and European Economic Area (EEA), before being rolled out in additional regions.
Featuring built-in biometric verification, fraud detection mechanism, real-time notifications and 3DS, the Solflare Card is designed to enable users to spend crypto as fiat, instantly, securely, and globally. By partnering with Mastercard, the Solflare Card will enable seamless stablecoin and crypto transactions at more than 150 million Mastercard-accepting merchants worldwide.
“Crypto is no longer simply a speculative asset class, and we want our 4 million+ strong community to use crypto in whatever way suits their daily needs”, said Filip Dragoslavic, Co-Founder and Co-CEO of Solflare. “With the Solflare Card, we are making it faster, safer, and easier to participate in the crypto economy. Crypto spending shouldn’t feel risky or overly complicated. The Solflare Card brings control back to users, with security and simplicity built-in.”
Christian Rau, senior vice president of Global Partnerships, Digital Assets, Blockchain and Fintech Enablement at Mastercard, said: "Mastercard is committed to integrating digital currencies into the broader payments ecosystem. By working with partners like Solflare, we further that mission and empower users through safe, simple and instant access to their crypto assets – truly bridging the gap between digital asset ecosystem and everyday payments.”
The Solflare Card is linked to users’ non-custodial Solflare wallet, meaning users maintain control of their private keys while Solflare manages the rails. This keeps users’ private keys secure, never exposed or transferred, while each transaction is signed directly (with the user’s approval) on the card, supported by three-factor authentication, including biometric verification, a PIN, and the physical card itself. Upon launch, Solflare cards are compatible with Google Pay, with Apple Pay coming soon, for fast, highly secure, contactless payments.
Vidor Gencel, Co-Founder and Co-CEO of Solflare, added, ““With over 115K of our loyal community members signed up for the

# MetaMask Announces Free Solana ID Minting for Users Before Breakpoint 2025
MetaMask is offering free Solana ID minting until December 22, unlocking exclusive Solana ecosystem perks for users.
![Kelvin Munene](https://secure.gravatar.com/avatar/d4ea74978aea7ee299447dc24a59aa1dac1fdc2a31a46fbf19da2cdce3d322bd?s=32&r=g)By[Kelvin Munene](https://coincentral.com/author/kelvin/)December 8, 2025**4 Mins Read
![](https://coincentral.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/08/metamask-1.png "MetaMask Announces Free Solana ID Minting for Users Before Breakpoint 2025")
## TLDR
* MetaMask users can mint a Solana ID for free until December 22, 2025.
* The initiative provides users access to perks like staking boosts and rewards.
* Solana partners like Orca and Sanctum offer exclusive benefits for Solana ID holders.
* The program aims to increase wallet adoption and engagement in Solana apps.
MetaMaskhas announced a new initiative that offers users the chance to mint a free Solana ID. This special promotion is available until December 22, 2025. The move is part of a broader partnership between MetaMask and Solana Identity, ahead of Solana’s annual developer conference, Breakpoint 2025. This initiative is designed to encourage users to engage more with Solana’s ecosystem and to provide them with exclusive benefits.
**Mint a Solana ID to Unlock Exclusive Ecosystem Perks**
By minting a Solana ID using MetaMask,[users](https://x.com/MetaMask/status/1998060127950409942?s=20)can unlock various perks across the Solana blockchain ecosystem. These include rewards like staking boosts, exclusive swaps, and access to discounts. Additionally, users will be eligible to participate in raffles and earn rewards from several key partners within the Solana network. Notable Solana-based platforms involved in the program include Orca, SNS, Sanctum, Cudis, and Play Solana.
MetaMask users will not only gain access to these platform-specific rewards but will also be able to explore further opportunities within the Solana ecosystem. These perks are meant to deepen user engagement and increase wallet adoption among MetaMask users who are interacting with Solana applications.
**How the Solana ID Minting Works**
Minting a Solana ID via MetaMask is straightforward. Users simply need to connect their[MetaMask](https://coincentral.com/metamask-introduces-musd-to-simplify-crypto-payments-and-defi-access/)wallet to the Solana Identity platform and follow the steps to mint their Solana ID. The process is free, and users will retain the ID throughout their interactions with Solana-based services. Once minted, the Solana ID will be linked to the user’s MetaMask wallet, allowing them to access the various ecosystem benefits.
The initiative aims to bring together the strengths of MetaMask’s popular walle

editorial.headline: Solflare Commoditizes Self-Custody With $49 NFC Hardware Wallet
editorial.standfirst: By stripping away screens and cables, Solflare is aggressively undercutting incumbent hardware providers to lower the barrier for retail security. This vertical integration signals a shift toward closed-loop, mobile-first security ecosystems on Solana.
key_takeaways: Solflare Shield launches immediately at a $49 price point, roughly 70% cheaper than standard hardware wallets.
The device utilizes an NFC-enabled offline chip with a 25-year warranty, eliminating batteries and cables.
Solflare enforces ecosystem lock-in by making the Shield exclusively compatible with the Solflare mobile app.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
In a direct challenge to the hardware wallet hegemony of **Ledger** and **Trezor**, **Solflare** has unveiled the **Solflare Shield**, a card-shaped cold storage device designed to radically simplify self-custody. With over $9.9 billion stolen from users in 2024 alone—often due to seed phrase phishing—the industry has long struggled to bridge the gap between high-security hardware and mass-market usability. Co-Founder **Filip Dragoslavic** argues that the current hardware stack is bloated, over-engineered, and priced out of reach for the average user, creating a dangerous friction point for adoption.

The **Shield** diverges from traditional architecture by removing the screen, battery, and Bluetooth connectivity, relying instead on Near Field Communication (NFC) to sign transactions via a simple tap against a smartphone. By offloading the user interface and threat simulation to the **Solflare** software wallet, the device reduces the bill of materials enough to launch at a $49 price point—a fraction of the $150 industry standard. This form factor eliminates the mechanical failure points of buttons and batteries, allowing **Solflare** to offer a 25-year warranty on the device.

Strategically, this move represents a significant pivot toward vertical integration within the **Solana** ecosystem. Unlike agnostic hardware wallets that strive to support every chain and interface, the **Shield** functions exclusively with the **Solflare** app. This "Apple-like" approach allows for tighter security integration—such as relying on the software's transaction simulation—but also serves as a powerful retention mechanism. By controlling both the software rails and the hardware key, **Solflare** is positioning itself not just as a wallet interface, but as a comprehensive security infrastructure provider for the next wave of retail capital.
